title: Create User - Happy Path with Valid Mandatory Details
description: Verify that an admin can successfully create a new user when all mandatory fields are provided with valid data. Confirms HTTP 201 response and correct response payload.
preconditions: Admin user has a valid JWT token with CREATE_USER permission.
No existing user with email john.doe@test.com in the system.

steps.action: Send POST request to /api/v1/users with required headers and payload.
steps.expected_result: Response status 201 Created and response body contains userId, message, status='ACTIVE', createdDate.

expected_result: User is created, unique userId is returned, status defaults to ACTIVE, audit fields createdBy (admin) and createdDate are populated.

title: Create User - Boundary Test for FirstName and LastName Length (50 chars)
description: Validate that the system accepts firstName and lastName exactly at the maximum allowed length of 50 characters.

steps.action: Send POST request to /api/v1/users with firstName and lastName each 50 characters long.
steps.expected_result: Response status 201 Created and user is created successfully.

expected_result: User created with provided max‑length names; no validation error is returned.

title: Create User - Duplicate Email Should Fail
description: Ensure that attempting to create a user with an email that already exists returns a conflict error.
preconditions: A user with email duplicate@test.com already exists in the system.

steps.action: Send POST request to /api/v1/users using an email that is already registered.
steps.expected_result: Response status 409 Conflict and errorCode USR_409 with message 'Email already exists'.

expected_result: User is not created; system returns appropriate error indicating email uniqueness violation.

title: Create User - Invalid Email Format
description: Verify that the API rejects a request when the email field does not conform to standard email format.

steps.action: Send POST request with malformed email address.
steps.expected_result: Response status 400 Bad Request with validation error indicating invalid email format.

expected_result: User is not created; validation error returned for email field.

title: Create User - Missing Mandatory Fields
description: Confirm that the API returns an error when one or more required fields are omitted from the request payload.

steps.action: Send POST request without the password field.
steps.expected_result: Response status 400 Bad Request with validation messages for missing password (and any other omitted mandatory fields).

expected_result: User creation fails; API reports all missing mandatory fields.

title: Create User - Unsupported Role Value
description: Validate that the system rejects a role that is not part of the supported role list.

steps.action: Send POST request with role set to an unsupported value.
steps.expected_result: Response status 400 Bad Request with error indicating unsupported role.

expected_result: User is not created; validation error returned for role field.

title: Create User - Password Policy Violation (Less Than 8 Characters)
description: Ensure that a password not meeting the minimum length requirement is rejected.

steps.action: Send POST request with password of 7 characters.
steps.expected_result: Response status 400 Bad Request with validation error indicating password length requirement.

expected_result: User creation fails; API returns password policy violation message.

title: Create User - Verify Audit Fields Persisted in Database
description: After successful user creation, confirm that audit fields (createdBy, createdDate) are correctly stored in the users table.
preconditions: Admin JWT token is valid and admin username is 'adminUser'.
Database connection is available for verification.

steps.action: Send POST request to create a new user with unique email.
Query the users table for the record with email sam.taylor@test.com.
steps.input_data: SELECT createdBy, createdDate FROM users WHERE email='sam.taylor@test.com';
steps.expected_result: createdBy equals 'adminUser' and createdDate is a recent timestamp (within 5 seconds of request).

expected_result: Audit fields are correctly persisted, confirming proper population of createdBy and createdDate.
